rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CECIL COREY DONALDSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine, in light of petitioner/successor administrator's declaration requesting a 90-day continuance and disclosing some additional tasks preventing this estate from closing and rendering this petition for final distribution premature. It should also be noted that nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner on 11/04/2025): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. 3) The first account period exceeds 2 years from the date of death, but no permission to exceed the 2-year period has been sought. See LRSF 14.34(A). 4) The deceased administrator's final account must be filed by his personal representative. If the personal representative does not account, the attorney for the deceased administrator or the current attorney of record must account. See Probate Code section 10953(b) & (c). Is petitioner, in her capacity as one of the attorneys of record (or one of the attorneys for the deceased administrator), joining in this petition including the deceased administrator's final account? 5) Paragraphs 16 & 17 & 20 (re IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)) of the petition must also address the deceased administrator, although allegations may be verified on information and belief. See section 10953(d). 6) A copy of letters issued to the beneficiary's personal representative and certified within 60 days before the hearing must be conventionally filed. See LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(6). 7) If the attorneys intend to add fees for more extraordinary services (in addition to those rendered to prepare the deceased administrator's final account), the petition must be amended with new notice. The net cash amount distributable to the beneficiary's personal representative must also be recalculated. 8) Is petitioner waiving fees for her services as successor administrator? See CRC 7.706(b) for additional requirements if petitioner is a partner or shareholder of the law firm serving as the attorneys of record. 9) Exhibit F must be revised to segregate time spent on preparation of the deceased administrator's final account, as time spent on preparation of petitioner's own account is part of ordinary services. See CRC 7.703(c)(11). If the deceased administrator's final account was prepared by a paralegal, a declaration required by CRC 7.703(e) must also be filed. 10) Please explain why no interest has been earned on the estate cash since petitioner was appointed as successor administrator. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT HOLT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. Decedent's Will contains a $50,000 pet trust clause naming Executor as trustee, which Executor argues is no longer needed due to the pet caretaker's "disclaimer". 2. Anne Lewis' waiver of account was executed by Jordan Holt acting under a United Kingdom Lasting Power of Attorney. Probate Code 4053 is cited for the proposition that a durable power of attorney executed in another jurisdiction is enforceable in California if it complies with the law of the jurisdiction where executed. However, Petitioner has not submitted evidence establishing that the power of attorney satisfies those requirements in the United Kingdom, nor has Petitioner provided adequate authentication of the power of attorney. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST AGREEMENT FOR THE BENEFIT OF CHONA ERGUIZA, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Co-Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/26. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. A copy of the order establishing the trust must be provided. Note: whether venue is proper in this county will be considered thereafter. 2. Petitioner requests that no bond be required but does not provide any support for the request. 3. Addresses were not provided in Par. 24 of the petition. 4. The trust states that accountings were required every 2 years with the court. Please address.
